commit.message:
Feat/saturn (#61)

* chore: update flatpakrepo URLs to castrojo fork

Point Url and Homepage to castrojo.github.io/testhub and
github.com/castrojo/testhub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add io.github.kolunmi.Saturn

Saturn is a GNOME GTK4/libadwaita Flatpak launcher and application
manager by Eva M (kolunmi). Built with Meson (C) and a Common Lisp
backend via ECL (Embeddable Common Lisp).

Build notes:
- ECL compiled from source (tag 24.5.10) — first build is slow
- 15 CL library dependencies, all pinned to specific commits
- Runtime: org.gnome.Platform//50
- x-skip-install-test: true for bootstrap (remove after first index commit)

Broad filesystem permissions (--filesystem=home, /var/lib/flatpak,
--talk-name=org.freedesktop.Flatpak) are justified for a Flatpak
manager application.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add finish-args-flatpak-system-folder-access lint exception

Saturn needs /var/lib/flatpak access (system-wide flatpak install dir)
since it's a Flatpak launcher/manager.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): rename app-id → id for flatpak-builder-lint exception resolution

flatpak-builder-lint's infer_appid() only reads the 'id' key, not
'app-id'. When appid cannot be inferred, exceptions are never applied
and all errors are reported unconditionally. Renaming to 'id' (the
canonical form accepted by both flatpak-builder and the linter) allows
our user-exceptions to take effect.

Root cause: flatpak_builder_lint/manifest.py infer_appid returns None
for manifests using the legacy 'app-id' key.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add appstream-missing-screenshots builddir lint exception

manifest-lint uses 'metainfo-missing-screenshots', builddir-lint uses
the distinct key 'appstream-missing-screenshots'. Both needed for Saturn
since we have no upstream screenshots yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add repo-stage screenshot lint exception

Three lint stages use three distinct exception keys for screenshots:
- manifest:  metainfo-missing-screenshots
- builddir:  appstream-missing-screenshots
- repo:      appstream-screenshots-not-mirrored-in-ostree

All three needed since Saturn has no screenshots yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): use app-id key instead of id

testhub's Justfile metadata recipe looks for 'app-id' key;
flatpak-builder accepts both 'id' and 'app-id' but testhub
convention (all other manifests) is 'app-id'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(justfile): support 'id:' as alias for 'app-id:' in manifest lookup

Newer Flatpak manifests use 'id:' as the canonical field (flatpak-builder
accepts both; flatpak-builder-lint's infer_appid() only reads 'id:').
Saturn uses 'id:' to allow user-exceptions to be resolved by app ID.

Updated all Justfile lookups to handle both keys:
- metadata recipe: falls back to '.id' when '.app-id' is empty
- _compile/_repack/run-test: yq '.".app-id" // .id' for direct reads
- grep-based directory lookups: '-lE (app-id|id): $APP'

All existing apps using 'app-id:' continue to work unchanged.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(saturn): remove x-skip-install-test bootstrap flag

Build 5 (run 23395028554) succeeded — Saturn is now indexed in the
testhub gh-pages repo. Real e2e install test can now run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(skills): capture Saturn onboarding lessons

- Add saturn to app-gotchas.md (ECL time, three-stage lint exceptions,
  app-id key requirement, x-skip-launch-check, bootstrap pattern)
- Add two-build bootstrap pattern to pipeline.md
- Add three lint stages section to pipeline.md
- Add manifest key convention (app-id: vs id:) to pipeline.md

Lessons from Saturn CI run 23395028554 (feat/saturn branch).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add x-skip-launch-check for headless CI

Saturn is a GTK4 GUI app — flatpak run exits 1 with 'Failed to open
display' in the headless gnome-50 container. x-skip-launch-check skips
the launch test while preserving the flatpak install verification.

Closes castrojo/testhub#6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(pr61): address Copilot review comments

- grep -lE -> grep -lF (4 locations): app IDs contain dots which are
  regex wildcards in ERE mode; -F fixed-string avoids accidental matches
- testhub.flatpakrepo: revert fork-specific castrojo URLs to projectbluefin
- build.yml: remove non-existent e2e-install from container comment
- docs: update app-id vs id guidance in pipeline.md, app-gotchas.md,
  and flatpaks/saturn/GOTCHAS.md to reflect Justfile now supporting both keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix/nightly builds (#60)

* chore: bump container image gnome-49 → gnome-50

GNOME 50 is the current stable release. Using the gnome-50 Flathub
infra image pre-caches the GNOME 50 SDK, improving build performance
for apps targeting org.gnome.Platform//50 (e.g. Saturn).

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(nightly): push to main directly + switch thunderbird to FTP URL

- Replace PR workflow with direct push to main. GITHUB_TOKEN cannot
  trigger other workflows via events, so we explicitly dispatch build.yml
  via 'gh workflow run' after pushing (requires actions:write permission).
  This eliminates the PR backlog that was blocking daily builds.

- Switch Thunderbird download from download-installer.cdn.mozilla.net to
  ftp.mozilla.org. The CDN caused HTTP/2 stream errors (curl exit code 92)
  on 2026-03-18, leaving the sha256 stale. FTP is the canonical stable source
  (same server Firefox nightly already uses).

- Add --retry 3 --retry-delay 5 to all curl downloads for resilience.

- Update thunderbird-nightly manifest sha256 to current value (force-refresh
  after CDN failure left it ~2 weeks stale).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(thunderbird-nightly): correct x86_64 sha256

Stale sha256 from previous session caused flatpak-builder to reject
the download. Correct value fetched from Mozilla's own checksums file:
ftp.mozilla.org/.../thunderbird-150.0a1.en-US.linux-x86_64.checksums

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): curl -f to fail on HTTP errors; drop e2e-install from comment

- curl -sL silently writes HTML error page on 404/503, producing a
  wrong sha256. Switch to curl -fsSL with explicit failure check.
- build.yml header comment referenced a non-existent e2e-install job.

Addresses Copilot review comments on PR #60.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------
